authority: Income Tax Department, Government of India
applicable_law: Income Tax Act, 1961 (as amended by Finance Act 2025)

new_regime.name: New Tax Regime (Default from FY 2023-24 onwards)
new_regime.rebate_87A: Tax rebate up to ₹60,000 for income up to ₹12,00,000 — effectively NIL tax up to ₹12 lakh
new_regime.standard_deduction: ₹75,000 for salaried employees and pensioners (effective income up to ₹12.75 lakh = NIL tax for salaried)
new_regime.best_for: Individuals with fewer investments/deductions. Simple and beneficial for most salaried employees.

old_regime.name: Old Tax Regime (Optional — must opt-in)

super_senior_citizen_slabs.note: For individuals aged 80 years and above

old_regime.rebate_87A: Tax rebate up to ₹12,500 for income up to ₹5,00,000
old_regime.best_for: Individuals with significant deductions under 80C, 80D, HRA, home loan interest exceeding ₹3.75 lakh

surcharge.10%: On tax if income is ₹50 lakh to ₹1 crore
surcharge.15%: On tax if income is ₹1 crore to ₹2 crore
surcharge.25%: On tax if income is ₹2 crore to ₹5 crore (new regime: capped at 25%)
surcharge.37%: On tax if income above ₹5 crore (old regime only; new regime capped at 25%)

section_80CCD1B.eligible: NPS (National Pension System) contribution by individual
section_80CCD1B.note: This is OVER and ABOVE the ₹1.5 lakh 80C limit — total benefit up to ₹2 lakh

section_80CCD2.limit: Up to 14% of Basic+DA for government employees; 10% for private sector
section_80CCD2.note: Available in both old and new regime — use this to reduce tax even under new regime

limits.self_spouse_children_senior_citizen: Up to ₹50,000 (if self/spouse is senior citizen)
limits.parents_senior_citizen: Up to ₹50,000 additional (if parents are senior citizens)
limits.preventive_health_checkup: Up to ₹5,000 within the above limits

section_24b.let_out_property: Full interest amount (no limit) — but set-off against other income capped at ₹2 lakh/year

section_80E.duration: 8 years from the year of first repayment or till interest is paid, whichever is earlier

section_80G.deduction: 50% or 100% of donation depending on organisation

section_80TTA.description: Interest from savings bank account (non-senior citizens)

section_80TTB.description: Interest income for senior citizens (FD + savings bank + post office)

HRA.description: House Rent Allowance exemption for salaried employees paying rent
HRA.calculation: Least of: (a) Actual HRA received; (b) Rent paid minus 10% of Basic Salary; (c) 50% of Basic+DA for metro cities, 40% for non-metro

LTA.description: Leave Travel Allowance — travel expenses within India for self and family
LTA.claim_frequency: 2 times in a block of 4 years (current block: 2022-2025)

debt_mutual_funds_post_april_2023.note: For debt MF units purchased on/after 1 April 2023
debt_mutual_funds_post_april_2023.tax_treatment: All gains taxed at slab rate regardless of holding period. No indexation benefit.

debt_mutual_funds_pre_april_2023.short_term: Less than 36 months — taxed at slab rate
debt_mutual_funds_pre_april_2023.long_term: 36 months or more — 20% with indexation benefit

long_term.rate: 12.5% without indexation (Finance Act 2024 removed indexation for property sold after 23 July 2024)
long_term.exception: Taxpayers can choose 20% with indexation for properties purchased before 23 July 2024 — whichever is beneficial

gold_physical.short_term: Less than 24 months — taxed at slab rate
gold_physical.long_term: 24 months or more — 12.5% without indexation (post 23 July 2024)

unlisted_shares.short_term: Less than 24 months — taxed at slab rate

tds_rates_common.threshold: ₹1 crore (₹20 lakh if no ITR filed for 3 years)

itr_forms.for: Salaried individuals + one house property + other income up to ₹50 lakh. Simplest form.
Individuals with capital gains, foreign income/assets, more than one house property, or income above ₹50 lakh.
Presumptive income scheme — small businesses/professionals with turnover up to ₹2 crore / ₹75 lakh.

important_deadlines_fy2025_26.activity: First advance tax instalment (15% of estimated tax)
ITR filing deadline for individuals (non-audit cases) for FY 2025-26
ITR filing deadline for audit cases / companies for FY 2025-26
Belated/revised ITR filing deadline for FY 2025-26

advance_tax.applicability: If total tax liability exceeds ₹10,000 in a year after TDS
advance_tax.who_is_exempt: Senior citizens (60+) with no business income are exempt from advance tax
advance_tax.interest_on_default: Interest under Section 234B (3% per month) and 234C (1% per month) if advance tax not paid

crypto_vda_tax.rate: 30% flat on profits from Virtual Digital Assets (crypto, NFTs)
crypto_vda_tax.no_deduction: No deduction allowed except cost of acquisition
crypto_vda_tax.no_loss_setoff: Losses from VDA cannot be set off against any other income
crypto_vda_tax.tds: 1% TDS on transfer of VDA above ₹50,000 per transaction (₹10,000 for specified persons)

new_regime_vs_old_regime_breakeven.note: Breakeven deduction level where both regimes give same tax
new_regime_vs_old_regime_breakeven.approximate_breakeven: If your total deductions (80C + 80D + HRA + home loan interest etc.) exceed ~₹3.75 lakh, old regime may be better. Otherwise new regime is beneficial.
new_regime_vs_old_regime_breakeven.recommendation: Use the IT department's tax calculator at incometax.gov.in to compare both regimes based on your actual income and deductions.

useful_links.form_26AS: Tax credit statement — shows all TDS deducted on your behalf. Available on income tax portal.
useful_links.AIS: Annual Information Statement — comprehensive view of all financial transactions. Available on income tax portal.

disclaimer: Tax laws change frequently. This information is for FY 2025-26 based on Finance Act 2025. Consult a Chartered Accountant or Tax Advisor for personalised advice.
